definition: The path factor of remembering rightly, keeping what matters in mind in a way aligned with the path.
notes: OSF house style now prefers right remembering based on the Dhammarato quotes draft, where the factor is explicitly framed in remembering language rather than as mindfulness. Right sati remains available in more source-facing technical contexts, while right noble remembering is retained for quote-book and header-style usage.

context_rules.context: In most path-factor and practical OSF contexts
In path-factor and source-facing doctrinal contexts that preserve more Pāli texture
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the Pāli term is intentionally retained.

context_rules.context: In Dhammarato quote-book headings or close paraphrase of that house idiom
context_rules.notes: Source-backed heading style, not required in every prose context.

context_rules.context: In Punnaji-style pedagogical exposition of the eightfold path
context_rules.notes: Retained as a named source-specific alternate.

example_phrases.notes: Closely related practice framework under current house style.
